name: Campi Flegrei
eruptionHistory: A supervolcano caldera. The Campanian Ignimbrite eruption 39,000 years ago was one of the largest in European history. Ongoing bradyseism (ground uplift) since 2005 has raised concern.
description: Campi Flegrei is a supervolcano caldera west of Naples. Ongoing ground uplift and seismic activity have raised alarm about a potential future eruption threatening the Naples metropolitan area.
